perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" shall mean: (a) All information disclosed by FormFactor to Recipient whether such information is disclosed in written, graphic, electronic, oral or sample form; and (b) All component specifications, component and contact structures, equipment designs, electronic configurations, manufacturing processes and methodologies, including any information which can be obtained by examination, testing, repair, reverse engineering and analysis of any hardware, or component part thereof comprising, relating to, or a part of a product manufactured or assembled with FormFactor's technology, notwithstanding the fact that the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have not been fulfilled.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" shall mean: (a) All information disclosed by FormFactor to Recipient whether such information is disclosed in written, graphic, electronic, oral or sample form; and (b) All component specifications, component and contact structures, equipment designs, electronic configurations, manufacturing processes and methodologies, including any information which can be obtained by examination, testing, repair, reverse engineering and analysis of any hardware, or component part thereof comprising, relating to, or a part of a product manufactured or assembled with FormFactor's technology, only if the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have been fulfilled.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1.1 to include the clause 'only if the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have been fulfilled,' it directly contradicts the original clause 'notwithstanding the fact that the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have not been fulfilled.' This creates ambiguity regarding whether unmarked information is considered confidential, directly affecting the enforcement of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" shall mean: (a) All information disclosed by FormFactor to Recipient whether such information is disclosed in written, graphic, electronic, oral or sample form; and (b) All component specifications, component and contact structures, equipment designs, electronic configurations, manufacturing processes and methodologies, including any information which can be obtained by examination, testing, repair, reverse engineering and analysis of any hardware, or component part thereof comprising, relating to, or a part of a product manufactured or assembled with FormFactor's technology, only if the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have been fulfilled.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 1.1 explicitly states that 'Confidential Information' includes information obtained through analysis of FormFactor's technology 'notwithstanding the fact that the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have not been fulfilled.' The changed text modifies this definition to include such information 'only if the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have been fulfilled.' This is a direct contradiction because the original says that marking is irrelevant to confidentiality, while the changed text makes it a condition. Since this contradiction occurs when comparing the Changed Text against the Contradicted Text, there is a clear contradiction that occurs due to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 As to any information which FormFactor regards as "Confidential Information", disclosures by FormFactor following the Effective Date are subject to and in FormFactor's sole and absolute discretion and will be made as follows: (a) If such information is in writing, or in a drawing, or in some other tangible form, such information at the time of such disclosure will be clearly marked as "Confidential Information"; and (b) In the event that such information is orally disclosed, as may happen during exchanges between the parties, FormFactor shall state that the information disclosed is Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 As to any information which FormFactor regards as "Confidential Information", disclosures by FormFactor following the Effective Date may or may not be marked as "Confidential Information" at FormFactor's discretion and will be made as follows: (a) If such information is in writing, or in a drawing, or in some other tangible form, such information at the time of such disclosure will be clearly marked as "Confidential Information"; and (b) In the event that such information is orally disclosed, as may happen during exchanges between the parties, FormFactor shall state that the information disclosed is Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 2.1 to state that information 'may or may not be marked' as confidential at FormFactor's discretion directly contradicts the subsequent clauses (a) and (b) which state that written information 'will be clearly marked' and oral information 'shall state' that the information disclosed is Confidential Information. This creates confusion about whether marking is mandatory or optional, and the original text specifies it 'will be made as follows' implying mandatory marking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 As to any information which FormFactor regards as "Confidential Information", disclosures by FormFactor following the Effective Date may or may not be marked as "Confidential Information" at FormFactor's discretion and will be made as follows: (a) If such information is in writing, or in a drawing, or in some other tangible form, such information at the time of such disclosure will be clearly marked as "Confidential Information"; and (b) In the event that such information is orally disclosed, as may happen during exchanges between the parties, FormFactor shall state that the information disclosed is Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.1 introduces a contradiction. Initially, it states that 'disclosures... may or may not be marked as "Confidential Information" at FormFactor's discretion'. This contradicts the subsequent clauses (a) and (b) within the same section which state that written information 'will be clearly marked' and oral information 'shall state' that the information disclosed is Confidential Information, because the word 'may or may not' directly conflicts with 'will' and 'shall'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely and exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement. Recipient shall not use Confidential Information for the benefit of any other party, or disclose, publish, disseminate or copy Confidential Information or any part thereof, to any other person, corporation or other organization without, in each case, obtaining the prior written consent of FormFactor.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely and exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement, unless explicitly authorized by Recipient's internal legal counsel. Recipient shall not use Confidential Information for the benefit of any other party, or disclose, publish, disseminate or copy Confidential Information or any part thereof, to any other person, corporation or other organization without, in each case, obtaining the prior written consent of FormFactor.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless explicitly authorized by Recipient's internal legal counsel' to Section 2.3 contradicts the rest of the section, which states that the Recipient shall only use the Confidential Information exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement, and only with FormFactor's consent. The added clause now allows Recipient's counsel to override FormFactor's control over the use of the information, creating uncertainty about the level of protection afforded to FormFactor's Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely and exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement, unless explicitly authorized by Recipient's internal legal counsel. Recipient shall not use Confidential Information for the benefit of any other party, or disclose, publish, disseminate or copy Confidential Information or any part thereof, to any other person, corporation or other organization without, in each case, obtaining the prior written consent of FormFactor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original agreement, allowing the Recipient's internal legal counsel to authorize the use of Confidential Information, even without FormFactor's consent, which directly contradicts the original intent and stated terms in the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text). This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding control over the Confidential Information and violates the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition because the clause is placed in a way that creates uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; provided, however, Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for any purpose. Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'provided, however, Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them' and replacing it with the phrase 'Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for any purpose' creates a direct contradiction. The original clause restricted the examination of products/samples to internal evaluation. The changed version allows for any type of examination, which would reasonably be interpreted as permitting reverse engineering and decompilation, which is strictly forbidden by the first clause. It is unclear whether examination of the product now allows actions that are forbidden in the first sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for any purpose. Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted the examination of products/samples to internal evaluation. The changed version allows for any type of examination, which would reasonably be interpreted as permitting reverse engineering and decompilation, which is strictly forbidden by the first clause of section 2.4. The contradicted text is section 2.4 itself, where the recipient 'shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information'. Therefore, the modified text creates a direct and strong contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by FormFactor with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later. All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by FormFactor with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, one (1) year following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is earlier. All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of changing the confidentiality term following a business relationship from 'five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later' to 'one (1) year following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is earlier' creates a contradiction. It is now unclear if it's a year or five years, which greatly changes the obligations of the involved parties. If a business relationship is terminated, it will cause a conflict between the 5 year agreement of confidentiality and the 1 year agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by FormFactor with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, one (1) year following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is earlier. All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of confidentiality after a business relationship terminates from 'five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later' to 'one (1) year following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is earlier'. This directly contradicts the initial agreement of 'five (5) years', creating a contradiction within the same section. The modified section (contradicted text) now has conflicting requirements and terms.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 This Agreement is the entire agreement between FormFactor and Recipient with respect to the subject matter contained herein and supersedes any prior or contemporaneously oral or written agreements concerning this subject matter. This Agreement may not be amended except by written agreement signed by authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 This Agreement is the entire agreement between FormFactor and Recipient with respect to the subject matter contained herein and supersedes any prior or contemporaneously oral or written agreements concerning this subject matter. This Agreement may be amended verbally between FormFactor and Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'This Agreement may be amended verbally between FormFactor and Recipient', it is contradicting the clause in the original text stating 'This Agreement may not be amended except by written agreement signed by authorized representatives of both parties'. It generates in-text contradiction as there is an uncertainy of whether the change should be done by verbal or written consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 This Agreement is the entire agreement between FormFactor and Recipient with respect to the subject matter contained herein and supersedes any prior or contemporaneously oral or written agreements concerning this subject matter. This Agreement may be amended verbally between FormFactor and Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. Initially, it states that the agreement can only be amended by written agreement. Then, the changed text specifies that it can be amended verbally, directly conflicting with the initial statement. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, where a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, within the same section) with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the amendment process.
